channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Lewa on Fire: No Euro club would do after Barça
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: New Chicago Fire signing Robert Lewandowski said he couldn't imagine playing for another European club after his four-season stint with Barcelona, and that explains in part his decision to move to MLS.

item.title: Man United sign Villa's Tielemans in $47M deal
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Manchester United have announced the signing of midfielder Youri Tielemans from Aston Villa.

item.title: Mbappé critical of tactics in loss by 'sloppy' Fra...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Kylian Mbappé seemed to question Didier Deschamps' approach and tactics after France's 2-0 defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup semifinal Tuesday.

item.title: England weighing man-marking plan to stop Messi
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: England coach Thomas Tuchel said he has considered man-marking Lionel Messi in Wednesday's World Cup semifinal against Argentina.

item.title: Scaloni: Argentina 'not as bad' as some people say...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni shut down criticism over the way his team has played throughout the FIFA World Cup ahead of the semifinal against England on Wednesday.

item.title: Spain 'feeling unbeatable' heading into WC final
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Luis de la Fuente said Spain are "feeling unbeatable" after reaching the World Cup final with a 2-0 win over France.

item.title: Matt Freese: USMNT criticism 'hurts my heart'
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: USMNT goalkeeper Matt Freese said he felt hurt by the backlash following the elimination from the 2026 World Cup after losing 4-1 to Belgium in the round of 16.

item.title: Balogun: Red card 'controversy' affected USMNT
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: United States striker Folarin Balogun revealed that he predicted President Donald Trump's involvement in overturning his World Cup suspension would "cause a lot of controversy."

item.title: 🥤 Why hydration breaks could be here to stay
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: FIFA's mandated three-minute breaks have been a talking point throughout the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Ashe declares readiness for Commonwealth Games
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian sprint star Favour Ashe declares mental and physical readiness for the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. Read more about Team Nigeria’s preparat
Read More: https://punchng.com/ashe-declares-readiness-for-commonwealth-games/

item.title: Lassa fever death toll hits 221 as NCDC records 922 confirmed cases
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigeria Centre for Disease Control and Prevention (NCDC) has confirmed that Lassa fever has claimed 221 lives across the country in 2026, with the number of confirmed infections rising to 922 as of the end of Epidemiological Week 26 (22nd - 28th June 2026). The post Lassa fever death toll hits 2

item.title: FG offers land to investors for mass housing projects
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government is offering land to credible investors for mass housing projects, aiming to address Nigeria’s 17 million housing deficit through pub
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-offers-land-to-investors-for-mass-housing-projects/

item.title: A’Court reserves judgment on ADC, 4 others’ bid to overturn deregistration order
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Court of Appeal sitting in Abuja yesterday heard and reserved judgment on appeals seeking to set aside the Federal High Court judgment that ordered the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) to deregister the African Democratic Congress (ADC) and four other political parties. The post

item.title: Free speech case with Kwara gov: Saraki to appeal decision on jurisdiction
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The case of criminalisation of free speech filed by the Governor of Kwara State, Mr Abdulrahman Abdulrazaq against former Senate President, Dr Abubakar Bukola Saraki came up yesterday at the Kwara State High Court sitting in Ilorin despite the absence of the lead defence lawyer, Mr. Jimoh Mumini, wh

item.title: NYSC threatens to blacklist hostile corps employers over welfare, accommodation
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The management of National Youth Service Corps (NYSC), has cautioned all corps employers to upgrade and prioritise the welfare of corps members posted to serve in their establishments, warning that any employer who did not provide accommodation and transportation for corps members would be blacklist

item.title: FAAN’s ride-booking app triggers airport taxi row
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
FAAN’s new ride-booking app, ACHRAMS, has sparked a major protest among airport cab drivers. Learn about the controversy and FAAN’s digital taxi reform.
Read More: https://punchng.com/faans-ride-booking-app-triggers-airport-taxi-row/

item.title: Fulham close in on Nwaiwu
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Fulham are nearing a deal for Super Eagles defender Chibuike Nwaiwu, but Trabzonspor is holding out for a higher transfer fee. Get the latest on Nwaiwu’s p
Read More: https://punchng.com/fulham-close-in-on-nwaiwu/

item.title: Police hunt killers of Kwara farmer found hacked to death
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kwara State Police launch a homicide investigation into the brutal killing of farmer Azeez Garba. Discover details of the search for his killers.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-hunt-killers-of-kwara-farmer-found-hacked-to-death/

item.title: Gombe gov hands INEC forms to APC assembly candidates
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Gombe State Governor Inuwa Yahaya presents INEC nomination forms to APC assembly candidates, declaring the party ready for the 2027 elections.
Read More: https://punchng.com/gombe-gov-hands-inec-forms-to-apc-assembly-candidates/

item.title: Amusan secures 2026 Diamond League final spot
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Tobi Amusan, world record holder, qualifies for the 2026 Wanda Diamond League Final in Brussels after consistent 100m hurdles performances.
Read More: https://punchng.com/amusan-secures-2026-diamond-league-final-spot/

item.title: Braga snaps up Inter Lagos teen
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Portuguese club Braga signs highly-rated Nigerian teen defender Adebisi Olamikun from Inter Lagos. Learn more about this exciting transfer.
Read More: https://punchng.com/braga-snaps-up-inter-lagos-teen/

item.title: NFF warns NPFL clubs ahead new licensing regime
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The NFF warns NPFL clubs to comply with new club licensing regulations for the 2026/27 season, backed by the National Sports Commission’s reforms.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nff-warns-npfl-clubs-ahead-new-licensing-regime/

item.title: FutbolConect eyes African football revolution
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Discover FutbolConect, the new digital platform connecting players, coaches, scouts, and clubs to revolutionize African football. Bridge talent gaps and ex
Read More: https://punchng.com/futbolconect-eyes-african-football-revolution/

item.title: Edo police rescue three kidnapped AAU students, kill suspect
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Edo State Police Command, on Tuesday, rescued three students of Ambrose Alli University (AAU), Ekpoma, who were abducted by gunmen in Ekpoma, Esan West Local Government Area of Edo State. The post Edo police rescue three kidnapped AAU students, kill suspect appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Land war in Delta
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: What was supposed to be a lifetime reward for community service has transformed into a living nightmare for Mr Victor Okolie. Popularly known in his hometown of Okpanam as 'Fuji,' the local contractor was gifted a tract of land by the leadership of the Ogbeozoma community in the Oshimili North Local

item.title: Nigerians finish in top 25 at East Africa Swing golf tournament
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Nigeria’s Kamalu Bako and Sunday Olapade posted respectable finishes at the East Africa Swing golf tournament, ending the competition inside the top 25 against a strong field dominated by Kenyan golfers. Bako emerged as the highest-placed Nigerian, finishing 11th with a three-round total of 210 afte

item.title: Okolode Yam Festival: A parade of culture in Ekpetiama Kingdom
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: On the final day of the Okolode New Yam festival (Aya buru fi uge), the aroma of roast yams is usually the first sign that welcomes you to Gbarantoru community in Ekpetiama Kingdom, Yenagoa Local Government Area of Bayelsa State. The whiffs of smoke, creating an alluring spectacle in the sky tell t

item.title: NSE schedules national engineering golf event for Oct 31
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Nigerian Society of Engineers (NSE) has fixed October 31 for the golf event of the 2026 National Engineering Games at the TYB International Golf Club, Yar’Adua Barracks, Abuja, as part of the second phase of the annual multi-sport competition. The golf tournament will be followed by the football

item.title: NOG energy week golf tour climaxes Thursday in Abuja
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The 25th anniversary edition of the NOG Energy Week Golf Tour will conclude on Thursday in Abuja, wrapping up four days of competition that serves as the traditional curtain-raiser to the annual NOG Energy Week Conference and Exhibition. The golf tournament, which teed off on Sunday, has drawn golfe

item.title: US star Balogun knew red card reversal would ‘cause a lot of controversy’
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Striker says FIFA's decision to suspend his one-match ban led to 'a lot of outside noise' before USA's knockout match.

item.title: Nigeria takes centre stage as Africa convenes on correctional excellence
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: There are moments in the life of a nation when history does not merely knock at the door; rather, it walks confidently through the gates, carrying with it the promise of transformation, prestige and renewed purpose. Such a moment beckons for Nigeria as the Nigerian Correctional Service (NCoS) prepa

item.title: The maltreatment of Professor Martin Aghaji
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: At what point in life or professional standing does one become immune to the indignities that can arise from the aggressive actions of overzealous law enforcement? Furthermore, what is the legal principle that asserts an individual is presumed innocent until proven guilty in a court of law? How can

item.title: France 0 -2 Spain Spain defeats France to reach final
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Mikel Oyarzabal scored from the penalty spot after a heady play by teenager Lamine Yamal, Pedro Porro added another goal and Spain advanced to its first World Cup final since winning in 2010 with a 2-0 victory over France on Tuesday. The post France 0 -2 Spain Spain defeats France to reach final ap

item.title: Trabzonspor powerless if Onuachu pushes for exit -Yilmaz
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Sports commentator and former Trabzonspor striker Mehmet Yılmaz has shared his thoughts on the growing speculation linking Nigerian forward Paul Onuachu with a move to Saudi Arabia during the current summer transfer window. Onuachu, who joined Trabzonspor permanently from Southampton last summer, en

item.title: Communities cry out over deplorable Asaba-Illah-Edo federal highway
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Residents of Ugbolu, Illah, Ebu and other communities on the Asaba -Ilah axis of the federal road linking Delta and Edo states have appealed for intervention by the authorities, following its total deterioration. The post Communities cry out over deplorable Asaba-Illah-Edo federal highway appeared f

item.title: Seven Britons among those killed in Spain wildfires
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Twelve of the 13 victims were foreign nationals, Spanish authorities said on Tuesday.

item.title: Publishers sue Google over alleged use of books to train AI models
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Book publishers sued Google on Tuesday, accusing the tech giant of illegally using copyrighted works to train its artificial intelligence models and generate content that competes with human authors. The lawsuit marks the latest legal battle over how AI developers use books and other creative works

item.title: MSF aircraft lands in Gusau Airport, boosts healthcare delivery
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: A Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) aircraft landed in Zamfara State, northwest Nigeria, for the first time following the opening of the new Gusau International Airport. This marks an important milestone in improving access to communities across the state and strengthening the delivery of healthcare an

item.title: Lawal shares 120 free trucks of fertiliser, 200,000 litres of herbicides, 100 crop residue crushers to farmers
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal distributed more than 120 trucks of fertilisers and essential farm inputs to Zamfara farmers. The Zamfara 2026 wet-season fertiliser programme was launched on Friday at the Trade Fair Complex in Gusau, the state capital. According to a statement by the governor’s spokesper

item.title: PenCom aims to improve police pensions, says exit from CPS not needed
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Johnbosco Agbakwuru ABUJA —THE The National Pension Commission, PenCom, has moved to allay rising unrest among Nigeria Police personnel about remaining in the Contributory Pension Scheme (CPS), saying it aims to secure improved retirement benefits for officers—not to back their withdrawal f

item.title: Iyanomo community women seek monarch’s intervention in leadership crisis
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Women of Iyanomo community in Ikpoba-Okha Local Government Area of Edo State have appealed to Governor Monday Okpebholo and the Oba of Benin, Oba Ewuare II, to intervene in the lingering leadership crisis The post Iyanomo community women seek monarch’s intervention in leadership crisis appeared fir

item.title: A special team beats brilliant individuals - how silky Spain flattened France
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Spain have rather flown under the radar at the 2026 World Cup - but will they go all the way and win it?

item.title: Brace for $4 gas again: How U.S.-Iran tensions are threatening to end the price break at the pump
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Americans got some relief at the gas pump in recent weeks, enough to slow down inflation. That might not last long as the U.S. and Iran continue to vie for control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Gbajabiamila calls on youths to champion national development
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Gbajabiamila said the Tinubu administration had confronted long-standing structural challenges through bold economic reforms, including the removal of fuel subsidy, the unification of the foreign exchange market and the modernisation of the country's tax system for the first time in a generation.

item.description: The proposal to end wasteful and ruinous gas flaring is welcome The disclosure by the German Ambassador to Nigeria and ECOWAS, Annett Günther, that Nigeria lost an estimated $1.5 billion

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: The current splintered opposition cannot challenge Tinubu in 2027, argues JACK OKUDE  Why is there a debate about who will win the 2027 Presidential election? It is no brainer. President

item.description: Beneficiaries of the Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) youth empowerment programme have expressed concern over persistent delays in the payment of their monthly stipends. item.description: Manchester United have completed the signing of Youri Tielemans from Aston Villa on a five-year deal. United have met a release clause of £35million for the 29-year-old midfielder, who has

item.title: FIFA Plans Closing Ceremony to Celebrate Historic Tournament
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: As the largest sporting event in history reaches its conclusion, the FIFA World Cup 2026™ closing ceremony, which will take place ahead of the final match at the New York

item.title: Nigeria’s worsening human rights crisis
item.dc:creator: The Sun Editorial
item.description: The National Human Rights Commission’s (NHRC) revelation that it received 326,113 human rights complaints in June alone is horrifying. This amounts to over 11,000 every single day. It is a devastating portrait of a nation where constitutional rights are increasingly under siege and where too many ci

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: As part of their build-up to the 14th Women Africa Cup of Nations finals which begin in Morocco on Sunday, Cup-holders Nigeria will do battle with Ghana in a friendly

item.dc:creator: ThisDay

item.title: LIVE: Trump says ‘strikes on Iran will continue until I say enough’
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Iran's bridges, power plants possible targets, Trump said as IRGC says it attacked US forces in Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan.

item.title: Trial of BFI Group, 7 others for alleged €100m fraud stalled
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: EFCC, is prosecuting the company alongside Reuben M. Jaja, Ozor Chidi Jerry, David Femi James, Imeobong Jumbo Udom, Adeolu Edward, Emeka Emmanuel Okorie, Johnson Gabriel (now at large) on amended five-count charges to the tune of €100 million (One Hundred Million Euros).

item.description: The Zamfara State Hospital Service Management Board (HSMB) successfully conducted a Computer-Based Test (CBT) for applicants seeking employment as Nurses and Midwives in the state healthcare system. The exercise was organised as part of the board’s commitment to ensuring a transparent, credibl

item.title: Celebrations over CTJ’s killing of bandit leader Sani Yellow in Zamfara

item.description: Citizens are celebrating the killing of a notorious bandit kingpin, Kachalla Sani Yellow, by a combined team of Civilian Joint Task Force, known as Askarawan Zamfara, and hunters in Zamfara State. The bandit warlord was killed in Anka Local Government Area of the State during an exchange of fire whi

item.title: Spain show teamwork may win out in 'World Cup of superstars'
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: France's attacking firepower was the focus of the build-up to their World Cup semi-final against Spain. If it wasn't Kylian Mbappe who did the damage, it would surely be Ousmane Dembele. Or Michael Olise. Or Bradley Barcola. Or Desire Doue.

item.title: ‘$200 million Zamfara Lithium Processing Plant to generate 2,000 jobs’
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal unveiled a $200 million lithium mining and processing plant, saying it will create 2,000 jobs for the citizens in the state. The mining plant was established in Boko, Zurmi Local Government Area of the state, and was commissioned by the governor at the weekend. He described the

item.title: Zamfara gov’t targets industrial boom with solid minerals, SME support

The Minister of Industry, Trade and Investment, Dr Jumoke Oduwole, stated this during a visit to Governor Dauda Lawal in Gusau, Zamfara, on Monday. The mi

item.description: Spain received strong performances up and down the lineup as they beat France to make the World Cup final.

item.description: The Mayor of Yenagoa City Council, Bulodisiye Ndiwari, has credited social media with the successful reunion of Mrs. Joy Izibevie Obugu with her family after 30 years of separation. item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: “We’ve given the French a proper trashing.” In Dallas, Spanish fans were delighted, whilst French fans were disappointed, following Spain’s 2-0 victory over France in the World Cup semi-final.

item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Spain have just won their World Cup semi-final against France. In the FIFA fan zone in Dallas, Spanish supporters celebrated this 2-0 victory.

item.title: Your vote is your voice, Issa-Onilu tells Osun electorate at NOA town hall with stakeholders
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “Every eligible voter in Osun State must understand that voting is not merely a political activity. It is a civic duty and a patriotic responsibility. The future of our communities, our state and our democracy depends on the choices we make at the polls."

item.description: The abrupt about-face from the US president was the latest twist in a conflict that has now lasted more than four months.

item.title: 'Extremely happy' Deschamps gets the farewell game no-one wants
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Didier Deschamps' long, storied career with France will come to an end not how he would have wanted it - in Saturday's World Cup third-place play-off.

item.title: US Congress takes next step to make daylight saving time permanent
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The House of Representatives passed a Trump-backed bill that would end decades of Americans having to reset their clocks twice a year.

item.title: FG Charges New NANS Leadership To Champion Safer Campuses
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government has challenged the newly inaugurated leadership of the National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) to champion safer campuses, protect students’ interests and promote constructive engagement with government and educational institutions. The charge was given at the i

item.title: WHO launches first trial of antiviral to prevent Bundibugyo strain of Ebola
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The World Health Organization launched the first clinical trial on Tuesday to test whether an antiviral drug can prevent infection in people exposed to the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo, as the outbreak continues to outpace response efforts and spread beyond known ch

item.title: KCSAPP Seeks Suspension Of Large Campaign Rallies In Kano Over Thug Violence
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A coalition of civil society organisations under the Kano Civil Society Alliance for Peaceful Politics (KCSAPP) has called for the suspension of large political campaign rallies in Kano State ahead of the 2027 general elections, citing growing concerns over political violence linked to thugs locally

item.title: World Cup 2026: Deschamps Laments ‘Poor Officiating’ In France Defeat To Spain
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: France coach Didier Deschamps questioned the officiating following France’s 2-0 World Cup semi-final defeat to Spain on Tuesday. While he admitted that his team was technically inferior and outplayed, Deschamps berated referee Iván Barton, asking reporters, “Is the referee at the level r

item.title: Nothing’s good-looking Watch 3 Pro smartwatch is just $69
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: While most fitness trackers are losing the screens to keep the price low, the CMF by Nothing Watch 3 Pro is a bit different. The budget-friendly smartwatch with a 1.43-inch OLED display is even cheaper than usual at Amazon, where it costs $69 in every color (the price fluctuates between $79 and $99)

item.title: Sanwo-Olu urges Lagos residents to plant trees, says state targets 20,000 this year
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Governor Sanwo-Olu reminded residents that anyone who unlawfully fells a tree is required under Lagos State law to replace it with five others. The post Sanwo-Olu urges Lagos residents to plant trees, says state targets 20,000 this year appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like a companion and become a physical manifestation of OpenAI’s ChatGPT."

item.title: Nigeria, US renew defence partnership to strengthen counter-terrorism, regional security
item.dc:creator: Ripples Nigeria
item.description: Nigeria and the United States have reaffirmed their commitment to expanding defence cooperation as both countries intensify efforts to combat terrorism, violent extremism and other evolving security threats across West Africa and the continent. The renewed commitment was highlighted during a courtes
